title: Brogy Mourns and Understands
description: On pages 9–12, brogy mourns and understands as the battle on Little Garden closes with the giants’ pride restored and the Straw Hats preparing to leave the ancient island.
scene: Brogy Mourns and Understands. The battle on Little Garden closes with the giants’ pride restored and the Straw Hats preparing to leave the ancient island. The focus is the story beat, character decisions, and dialogue driving this part.

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: Seems I've passed out for a bit...

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Even Elbaf's weapons couldn't withstand all the stress,

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: of a duel between giants that's been going on for a hundred years...

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: Don't hug Me So Much, Brogy, You're Hurting My Wounds...

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: Thank God you've survived, my dearest friend! Gababababababababababababababababababababababababababababababababababa!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: If anything, it's a miracle those weapons still look like weapons even after 100 years of constant usage.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Not to mention the Wellders themselves still wanting to fight after so long...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: What a splen- did day this is!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Let us thank the God of Elbaf!

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: Ha. Are you that happy about finally landing a blow on me,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: You know that's not the reason, you big ol' fool!

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: I intend to crush you flat, alright!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Why're you starting a fight already!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: didn't Vivi say that Mr. O was the enemy boss' codename...?

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: which would mean that the man on the other side of this denden-mushi right now is...!

summary: This part centers on brogy mourns and understands, clarifying the immediate conflict and how it moves the chapter toward its next major turn.
